recommendedJudge.primary: Claude Code session (Opus-class) running the two-pass grade + adversarial-verify flow — how every current score was produced

scalability: Append-only. Adding a model grades ONLY the new submission against the fixed calibration anchors in grading/PROMPT.md — existing scores are immutable and never recomputed, so the leaderboard stays reproducible and cost is O(1) per addition. Calibration lives as static anchors in the repo, not a live relative computation.
reGrade: Re-grade the whole set together ONLY when bumping rubricVersion (a deliberate, breaking change to the scale). Then update every submission's grading.rubricVersion. Scores across different rubricVersions are not directly comparable.
provenance: Every submission records grading.gradedBy / gradedOn / rubricVersion so drift between sessions or judges is detectable.
